title: Parameter kinds and argument binding

sources.locator: The Python Tutorial; Python 3.14; Tutorial §4.9 and chapter 8: function definitions, parameter rules, and errors; atomic topic: Parameter kinds and argument binding
sources.claim: Python binds arguments to positional-only, positional-or-keyword, variadic, keyword-only, and defaulted parameters according to the signature before the function body executes.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Python binds arguments to positional-only, positional-or-keyword, variadic, keyword-only, and defaulted parameters according to the signature before the function body executes.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A function uses None as a default and creates a new list per call, while a keyword-only timeout prevents an unclear positional boolean or number. Your goal is to trace argument binding and repair an unsafe or ambiguous function signature. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Core collections and text; pf abstraction calls; Function contracts and returns; Mutability, aliases and copying. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Python binds arguments to positional-only, positional-or-keyword, variadic, keyword-only, and defaulted parameters according to the signature before the function body executes. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Default expressions run once at definition, *args is a tuple, **kwargs is a dict, duplicate bindings fail, and changing a public parameter name can break keyword callers. The learner must reject this documented misconception: Default list expressions run for each call, so appending to a default cannot affect a later invocation.

includes: Use positional, keyword, default, variadic and keyword-only parameters without mutable-default traps.
Trace argument binding and repair an unsafe or ambiguous function signature.
excludes: Default expressions run once at definition, *args is a tuple, **kwargs is a dict, duplicate bindings fail, and changing a public parameter name can break keyword callers.

outcomes: Trace argument binding and repair an unsafe or ambiguous function signature.

examples.title: Parameter kinds and argument binding: direct language case
examples.situation: A function uses None as a default and creates a new list per call, while a keyword-only timeout prevents an unclear positional boolean or number.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A function uses None as a default and creates a new list per call, while a keyword-only timeout prevents an unclear positional boolean or number.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Default expressions are evaluated once at function definition; use an immutable sentinel such as None and create per-call mutable state inside the body.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Default expressions are evaluated once at function definition; use an immutable sentinel such as None and create per-call mutable state inside the body.

examples.title: Parameter kinds and argument binding: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A logging wrapper forwards *args and **kwargs exactly once and does not silently consume a caller keyword intended for the wrapped function.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A logging wrapper forwards *args and **kwargs exactly once and does not silently consume a caller keyword intended for the wrapped function.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Default expressions run once at definition, *args is a tuple, **kwargs is a dict, duplicate bindings fail, and changing a public parameter name can break keyword callers.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: Default list expressions run for each call, so appending to a default cannot affect a later invocation.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Trace argument binding and repair an unsafe or ambiguous function signature.

misconceptions.correction: Default expressions are evaluated once at function definition; use an immutable sentinel such as None and create per-call mutable state inside the body.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: Default list expressions run for each call, so appending to a default cannot affect a later invocation.

recap.summary: You should now be able to trace argument binding and repair an unsafe or ambiguous function signature. Keep this boundary in view: Default expressions run once at definition, *args is a tuple, **kwargs is a dict, duplicate bindings fail, and changing a public parameter name can break keyword callers.
